dart.name: Supabase Auth (via supabase_flutter)
dart.context: Managed auth with email, magic links, social logins. Native Flutter package.

dart.name: Firebase Auth
dart.context: Google's managed auth. Excellent Flutter integration. Free for most use cases.

typescript.name: Supabase Auth (via @supabase/supabase-js)
typescript.context: Works with React Native via the JS client. Same auth as web.

typescript.name: Firebase Auth (via @react-native-firebase)
typescript.context: Native Firebase integration for React Native. Well-supported.

kotlin.name: Firebase Auth
kotlin.context: Native Android SDK. Best-in-class for Kotlin Android apps.

kotlin.name: Supabase Auth (via supabase-kt)
kotlin.context: Kotlin Multiplatform client for Supabase. Good if already using Supabase.

default.name: Firebase Auth or Supabase Auth
default.context: Both provide managed auth with mobile SDKs. Firebase has deeper mobile integration. Supabase is simpler.

dart.name: Flutter
dart.context: Cross-platform from a single codebase. Strong typing, hot reload, large widget library.

typescript.name: React Native (Expo)
typescript.context: Managed workflow with Expo. Fastest path to app stores. Limited native module access.
typescript.when: Most React Native apps, especially first-timers

typescript.name: React Native (bare)
typescript.context: Full native module access. More setup, more control. Choose this if Expo's limitations block you.

kotlin.name: Kotlin + Jetpack Compose
kotlin.context: Android-native with modern declarative UI. Best performance on Android.

default.name: Flutter or React Native (Expo)
default.context: Both are strong cross-platform options. Flutter for Dart, React Native for TypeScript/React developers.

default.name: Offline tolerant
default.context: App works online but handles connectivity loss gracefully. Shows cached data, queues actions.

default.name: Offline capable
default.context: Core features work without connectivity. Syncs when online. Requires local database.

default.name: Offline first
default.context: Everything works offline. Network is optional. Significant complexity for sync and conflict resolution.

default.name: Supabase (PostgreSQL) + local SQLite cache
default.context: Cloud database with local caching for offline support. Best of both worlds.
default.when: Most mobile apps with cloud sync

default.name: Firebase Firestore
default.context: Real-time NoSQL with built-in offline persistence. Excellent mobile SDK.

default.name: SQLite only (local)
default.context: No cloud sync. All data stays on device. Simplest architecture.
